entries.title: Common GraphQL endpoint paths
entries.body:
/v1/explorer
/v1/graphiql
/graph
/graphql
/graphql/console/
/graphql.php
/graphiql
/graphiql.php

entries.title: Where to look and how to fingerprint the endpoint
entries.body:
GraphQL endpoints often live on predictable paths. The most common ones:
• /graphql
• /graphiql - an interactive IDE
Always check both the API and the dev/debug interfaces.
Extended wordlist: SecLists/Discovery/Web-Content/graphql.txt.

entries.title: Finding the injection point - GET endpoint
Finding the injection point - POST endpoint
Checking error visibility (broken queries)
Diagram: GraphQL returns login and password (HTB Help)

meta.caption: The request GET /graphql?query={user{username,password}} returns, in JSON, the username "helpme@helpme.com" and the password hash. Example from HTB Help - the data is served without authorization. Source: PayloadsAllTheThings.

entries.title: Minimal introspection query
How introspection works
entries.body:
The GraphQL specification has the service fields __schema and __type - they let the client ask the server which types exist, which fields they return and how everything is related.
This is exactly what powers autocompletion and inline documentation in GraphiQL and GraphQL Playground: the tool does not guess, it asks the server in advance what is valid.
For an attacker, introspection is a full dump of the schema: types, fields, arguments, mutations.

entries.title: Full schema dump (IntrospectionQuery, URL-encoded)
entries.body: fragment+FullType+on+__Type+{++kind++name++description++fields(includeDeprecated%3a+true)+{++++name++++description++++args+{++++++...InputValue++++}++++type+{++++++...TypeRef++++}++++isDeprecated++++deprecationReason++}++inputFields+{++++...InputValue++}++interfaces+{++++...TypeRef++}++enumValues(includeDeprecated%3a+true)+{++++name++++description++++isDeprecated++++deprecationReason++}++possibleTypes+{++++...TypeRef++}}fragment+InputValue+on+__InputValue+{++name++description++type+{++++...TypeRef++}++defaultValue}fragment+TypeRef+on+__Type+{++kind++name++ofType+{++++kind++++name++++ofType+{++++++kind++++++name++++++ofType+{++++++++kind++++++++name++++++++ofType+{++++++++++kind++++++++++name++++++++++ofType+{++++++++++++kind++++++++++++name++++++++++++ofType+{++++++++++++++kind++++++++++++++name++++++++++++++ofType+{++++++++++++++++kind++++++++++++++++name++++++++++++++}++++++++++++}++++++++++}++++++++}++++++}++++}++}}query+IntrospectionQuery+{++__schema+{++++queryType+{++++++name++++}++++mutationType+{++++++name++++}++++types+{++++++...FullType++++}++++directives+{++++++name++++++description++++++locations++++++args+{++++++++...InputValue++++++}++++}++}}

entries.title: Full schema dump (IntrospectionQuery, URL-decoded)
Schema dump in one line without fragments
entries.body: __schema{queryType{name},mutationType{name},types{kind,name,description,fields(includeDeprecated:true){name,description,args{name,description,type{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name}}}}}}}},defaultValue},type{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name}}}}}}}},isDeprecated,deprecationReason},inputFields{name,description,type{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name}}}}}}}},defaultValue},interfaces{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name}}}}}}}},enumValues(includeDeprecated:true){name,description,isDeprecated,deprecationReason,},possibleTypes{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name}}}}}}}}},directives{name,description,locations,args{name,description,type{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name,ofType{kind,name}}}}}}}},defaultValue}}}

entries.title: Schema dump in one line with fragments
entries.body: {__schema{queryType{name}mutationType{name}subscriptionType{name}types{...FullType}directives{name description locations args{...InputValue}}}}fragment FullType on __Type{kind name description fields(includeDeprecated:true){name description args{...InputValue}type{...TypeRef}isDeprecated deprecationReason}inputFields{...InputValue}interfaces{...TypeRef}enumValues(includeDeprecated:true){name description isDeprecated deprecationReason}possibleTypes{...TypeRef}}fragment InputValue on __InputValue{name description type{...TypeRef}defaultValue}fragment TypeRef on __Type{kind name ofType{kind name ofType{kind name ofType{kind name ofType{kind name ofType{kind name ofType{kind name ofType{kind name}}}}}}}}

entries.title: Schema enumeration via suggestions
Suggestions and brute-force when introspection is disabled
entries.body:
If you enter an unknown field, the GraphQL backend often returns a suggestion with a similar name from the schema (Did you mean ...?).
This enables schema enumeration even when introspection is disabled.
When the schema is unavailable, you can brute-force known field and type names with wordlists like Escape-Technologies/graphql-wordlist.

entries.title: Enumerating a type definition (using User as an example)
entries.body: {__type (name: "User") {name fields{name type{name kind ofType{name kind}}}}}

entries.title: Finding paths to a target type (graphql-path-enum)
entries.body:
graphql-path-enum -i ./test_data/h1_introspection.json -t Skill
Found 27 ways to reach the "Skill" node from the "Query" node:
- Query (me) -> User (pentester_profile) -> PentesterProfile (skills) -> Skill
- Query (query) -> Query (skills) -> Skill

entries.title: Why find paths to a type
entries.body:
After introspection it is not always obvious how to reach the type you want (User, Admin, Payment) - an object may be reachable through several entry points and nested relations.
graphql-path-enum takes the JSON output of introspection, analyzes the type relations and outputs all query paths that can reach the target object, even one that is deeply nested or exposed indirectly.

entries.title: Basic query (shorthand)
Basic query (full syntax with query)
Query with arguments (entry point for IDOR)
Nested query (traverse relations in a single request)
Notes on queries and IDOR
entries.body:
GraphQL supports three operation types: queries, mutations, subscriptions.
• The query structure mirrors the JSON response structure exactly: you request only the fields you need.
• Field arguments work like function parameters (id, filters, search) - this is a frequent entry point for testing access control and IDOR.
• Nested queries let you traverse relations in a single request (user -> posts -> comments) instead of a chain of REST calls.

entries.title: Mutation - login and user creation
Notes on mutations
entries.body:
A mutation is an operation that changes data on the server (create / update / delete).
Mutations work like functions: you use them to interact with the GraphQL endpoint.
Important: mutations usually do NOT work over GET (graphql-over-http issue #123).

entries.title: Batching via a JSON array of queries
Batching by query name
Brute-force via aliases in a single mutation
Why batching and aliases
entries.body:
GraphQL batching lets you send several queries in one HTTP request (an array of operations in the body). The server processes each operation and returns an array of responses.
Typical abuse scenarios:
• Amplifying password brute-force (many attempts in one request).
• Bypassing rate limiting.
• Bypassing 2FA.
Aliases (second:, third: ...) let you call the same mutation many times in one request - the same effect for brute-force.

entries.title: NoSQL injection via $regex in the search parameter
SQL injection - breaking out with a single quote
SQL injection inside a GraphQL field (stacked + sleep)
Note on SQLi/NoSQLi in GraphQL
entries.body:
GraphQL is just a layer between the client and the database, so SQL and NoSQL injections are still possible.
Try to push a payload into any resolver parameter (id, name, search, filters).
• SQLi: a single quote ' to break out, then stacked queries and time-based (pg_sleep).
• NoSQLi: JSON operators like $regex inside string filter parameters.

entries.title: Tools for attacking GraphQL
entries.body:
GraphQLmap - an engine for interacting with a GraphQL endpoint during a pentest.
InQL (doyensec) - a Burp extension for GraphQL security testing.
GQLSpection - parses the introspection schema and generates possible queries.
graphql-path-enum - enumerates the ways to reach a given type in the schema.
clairvoyancex - recovers the GraphQL schema when introspection is disabled.
CrackQL - GraphQL password brute-force and fuzzing.
graphql-cop - a security auditor for GraphQL APIs.
graphw00f - fingerprints the GraphQL server engine.
graphql-voyager - visualizes any GraphQL API as an interactive graph.
